## Source Record

- Source URL: `https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/get-started/what-is-github-copilot`
- Fetch method: `webfetch` in markdown mode
- Capture date: `2026-04-14`

## Why It Matters

This is the simplest authoritative statement of what GitHub Copilot is. It defines Copilot as an AI coding assistant, lists the major product surfaces, and distinguishes which agentic features are limited to higher-tier plans.

## Key Excerpts

> "GitHub Copilot is an AI coding assistant that helps you write code faster and with less effort."

> Copilot can provide code suggestions, chat help, command-line help, Copilot Spaces context, pull request summaries, and agentic code change workflows.

> The page states that research, planning, code changes, and pull request creation are available in `Copilot Pro+`, `Copilot Business`, and `Copilot Enterprise` only.

> Copilot is available across `your IDE`, `GitHub Mobile`, `Windows Terminal Canary`, `the command line`, and `the GitHub website`.

## Capture Notes

- This page is a concise product-definition page rather than a deep technical reference.
- It is useful for grounding later notes about plans, surfaces, and feature boundaries in GitHub's own summary language.
- The page also links outward to feature and setup docs, making it a clean entry point for further capture work.
